name: Savory Chicken Potpies
recipeIngredient: 5 lb Roasting chicken (OR two 2 lb Small carrots, pared, cut into half-inch long pieces
1 large Onion, peeled and quartered 1/2 pk Frozen pearl onions, cut into diagonal slices
3 Leafy celery tops 2 lg Celery stalks, cut into diagonal cuts
2 cup All-purpose flour, sifted 1 lg Egg yolk, beaten with 1 Tbsp water
recipeInstructions:
STOCK:
1. Place chicken in a 6-quart kettle with the rest of stock ingredients. Bring to a boil over high heat.
2. Reduce heat; simmer, covered, for about 1 hour, or until chicken is tender (about 45 minutes with 2 smaller chickens).
3. Meanwhile, make pastry: In a medium bowl, stir flour and salt together. With a pastry blender, cut in shortening until the mixture resembles coarse cornmeal.
4. Sprinkle ice water at a time over all of the pastry mixture, tossing lightly with a fork after each addition and pushing dampened portion to the side of the bowl. Add only dry portion remaining.
5. The pastry should be just moist enough to hold together, not sticky (4-6 tablespoons ice water). Shape into a ball; flatten and wrap in plastic wrap; refrigerate until ready to use.
6. Remove chicken from kettle to a bowl; cool until easy to handle. Discard skin and bones. Cut chicken meat into 1-inch pieces (about 4-5 cups).
7. Strain stock into a large saucepan. Bring to a boil. Add carrots, pearl onions, and celery. Cook, covered, for 5-10 minutes, until vegetables are just tender.
8. In a 2-cup measuring cup, measure milk; whisk in flour, poultry seasoning, salt, and pepper until smooth. Stir into vegetable-stock mixture; bring to a boil, stirring constantly.
9. Reduce heat; boil 1 minute. Add cut-up chicken; mix well. Divide mixture among six 1-1/3-cup casseroles or separate baking dishes.
10. Preheat oven to 400°F. Remove pastry from the refrigerator. On lightly floured surface, cut pastry into 6 equal pieces. Flatten and evenly roll each piece to a circle the same diameter as the top of a casserole.
11. Cut a cross, 1-1/2 inch across, in the center of each. Lightly moisten edges of casseroles with a pastry brush and water.
12. Place pastry circles on top of each casserole. Use knife tip to turn back the four corners in the center of the pastry, exposing filling.
13. Firmly press edge of pastry onto casserole with a table fork. Place casseroles on a large baking tray or rimmed cookie sheet.
14. Bake for 30 minutes or until filling is bubbly. Remove from oven. Brush crusts with egg yolk mixture.
15. Return to oven; bake for 5 minutes longer, or until pastry is golden-brown.
Makes 6 servings.
